questions.prompt: Why use environment-scoped secrets instead of repo-level?

choices.text: Environment-scoped secrets let you have the SAME secret name (e.g., DATABASE_URL) with DIFFERENT values per environment — staging uses staging-db-url, production uses prod-db-url, dev uses dev-db-url. The workflow references `${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}` and gets the right value based on which environment the job runs in. Prevents accidental cross-environment access (a staging deploy can't accidentally use the prod DB). Pair with required reviewers on the production environment for human-in-the-loop deploys. Repo-level secrets are shared across all workflows; environment secrets are scoped — much safer.

questions.explanation: Same name, different values per environment. Prevents cross-environment leaks; enables required reviewers.

questions.prompt: Library tests multiple Node versions. CI runs 5 minutes per matrix combo; 3 versions × 3 OS = 9 combos = 45 minutes. Speed-up?

choices.text: Matrix builds run in PARALLEL by default — all 9 combos start simultaneously on 9 runners. Total wall time is max(any combo) ≈ 5 minutes, not 45. Add caching via `cache: 'npm'` so each combo's npm install runs from cache; per-combo time drops to maybe 2 minutes. Disable `fail-fast: false` if you want all combos to complete on failure for full visibility (default is fail-fast: true which cancels remaining combos on first failure — useful for app CI where any failure blocks). 45 sequential minutes turns into ~2 parallel minutes with caching. Standard pattern for libraries with broad compatibility testing.

questions.explanation: Matrix runs in parallel by default. Add caching; total time becomes max(any combo).
